methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Cascade Space is a seed-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $5.9M to date. The valuation universe spans 23 private peers and 6 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL), though narrow peer matching proved limited at the seed stage.

Only one valuation method yielded sufficient data. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, produces an implied valuation of $15M when anchored to the $6M total raised proxy. This method assumes standardized progression mechanics across the space sector, which is reasonable but relies on historical stage-to-stage transitions rather than company-specific metrics. Peer-median analysis was not applicable: while 23 same-sector companies exist, the seed-stage and valuation-tier filters yielded insufficient comparable datapoints (fewer than three). Public-comp anchoring could not proceed due to absence of target revenue disclosure, a standard constraint for private company analysis; the sector median P/S of 45.3x across five public tickers would require revenue data to be actionable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M to $17M, centered on $15M, derived solely from stage-progression mechanics. Key uncertainties are material: the single-method foundation limits confidence, peer comparables at seed stage remain sparse, and the absence of revenue, unit economics, or technology maturity prevents revenue-multiple or cash-flow approaches. A broader dataset or updated financial disclosure would materially improve precision.
